disclaimer: For nursing training and supervision only — not a medical diagnosis.

side_lying_position.description: Body is rotated laterally to a true side-lying position (30–60° from supine). Assessed via shoulder/hip depth asymmetry and vertical tilt.

shoulder_hip_alignment.description: Shoulder line and hip line are parallel — no spinal axial twist that could cause pressure injury or discomfort.

head_support.description: Head is in a neutral cervical position. Ear aligned with shoulder — no hyperflexion or hyperextension of the neck.

back_support.description: Spine is relatively straight — shoulder midpoint, hip midpoint, and knee midpoint are collinear. Prevents scoliotic positioning.

leg_separation_support.description: Knees and ankles are separated, reducing pressure between bony prominences. A pillow between legs is the clinical standard.

pressure_area_protection.description: Heels, ankles, and dependent bony prominences are not compressed. Assessed via heel/ankle depth positioning and landmark visibility.

clinical_notes: Reposition patient every 2 hours to prevent pressure ulcers.
Ensure all bony prominences (sacrum, trochanters, heels, ankles, ears) are padded.
A 30° lateral tilt is preferred over 90° to reduce trochanteric pressure.
Document repositioning time, position, and skin condition after each turn.
This AI assessment supplements — but does not replace — direct clinical observation.
